text.title: One Task → One Branch → One Worktree → One Agent (Runtime-Isolated) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the task-worktree-runtime-isolation methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each scheduled task MUST get its own `git worktree add` directory and a dedicated feature branch. Reusing a worktree across tasks is forbidden.

rationale.source: git docs (man git-worktree) + parallel-agent harness audit

rule.rationale: Sharing a worktree races on the index and working tree; the only safe primitive for concurrency is one worktree per agent.

rule.statement: Every worktree MUST ship with a manifest naming the paths the agent may edit. Writes outside the manifest MUST be rejected by a pre-commit hook or harness wrapper.
rule.rationale: Without a manifest, agents quietly modify shared files and merge conflicts blow up at integration.

rule.statement: Each worktree MUST receive a distinct port range allocated by the harness (e.g., 4000 + 100 × worktree_index). Hard-coded ports in code are flagged.
rule.rationale: Two agents both bind to 4000 → second fails to boot, the agent retries, then `kills` the first agent's server. Port allocation must be central.

rule.statement: Each worktree MUST use a dedicated DB schema or database name (`appname_wt_<n>`) and a dedicated cache key prefix. Migrations run inside the worktree's namespace, never the shared one.
rule.rationale: Shared DB → migration in worktree A corrupts data in worktree B. Per-worktree schemas isolate state without spinning up new containers.

rule.statement: Each worktree MUST receive its own secret bundle (env file or Vault path). Reading the shared production bundle from a worktree is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Parallel agents reading the same secret bundle either rotate counters out from under each other or, worse, write production logs from a feature branch.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
